title: Gas Hydrates
focus: Characteristics, locations, and significance of gas hydrates as an unconventional hydrocarbon source.
rationale: A distinct scientific and geographical topic regarding unconventional energy sources, highly relevant for UPSC environment and geography questions.
sectionExcerpt: Gas hydrates are crystalline solids composed of water and gas, containing large amounts of methane. They are classified as unconventional hydrocarbons because extracting them requires advanced technologies. They exist in huge quantities in marine sediments below the sea floor and within the permafrost layer in the Arctic. In India, huge quantities are found around the Andaman Islands and in Krishna-Godavari offshore. China is constructing the world's first permanent undersea research station to study them.
evidenceBullets: Gas hydrates are crystalline solids composed of water and gas that contain large amounts of methane.
They are classified as unconventional hydrocarbons requiring advanced and non-traditional extraction technologies.
Found in marine sediments directly below the sea floor and within the Arctic permafrost layer.
In India, significant quantities are located around the Andaman Islands and in the Krishna-Godavari offshore basin.
China is building the world's first permanent undersea research station to study gas hydrates.

title: International Energy Agency (IEA)
focus: Genesis, membership criteria, and flagship reports of the International Energy Agency.
rationale: A major international organization with specific, testable membership criteria (like OECD prerequisite and 90-day reserves) and important flagship reports.
sectionExcerpt: The International Energy Agency (IEA), located in Paris, France, was founded in 1974 to ensure the security of oil supplies. It has 32 Member countries and 13 Association countries, with India being an associate member. To be a full member, a country must be an OECD member, maintain crude oil reserves equivalent to 90 days of the previous year's net imports, and have a demand restraint programme to reduce oil consumption by up to 10%. Flagship publications include the World Energy Outlook, Global Methane Tracker, Delivering Sustainable Fuels, and Global EV Outlook.
evidenceBullets: IEA was founded in 1974 to ensure the security of oil supplies and is headquartered in Paris, France.
India is an associate member of the IEA, not a full member.
Full membership requires OECD membership, accessible crude oil reserves equivalent to 90 days of the previous year's net imports, and a 10% demand restraint programme.
Flagship publications include the World Energy Outlook.
Recent reports include the Global Methane Tracker 2025, Delivering Sustainable Fuels, and Global EV Outlook 2025.

title: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics (ICRISAT)
focus: Genesis, purpose, and key achievements of ICRISAT.
rationale: A distinct agricultural research organization with specific achievements like the pigeon pea hybrid and Africa Food Prize, making it a prime candidate for agriculture-related questions.
sectionExcerpt: ICRISAT is a non-profit research centre established in the 1970s under the CGIAR consortium, located in Hyderabad, Telangana. Its purpose is to improve food security in semi-arid regions and support smallholder farmers in drylands globally. Key achievements include being awarded the Africa Food Prize in 2021 and developing the world's first pigeon pea hybrid. Recently, ICRISAT collaborated with RIS to launch the ICRISAT Centre of Excellence for South-South Cooperation.
evidenceBullets: Located in Hyderabad, Telangana, and established as a non-profit research centre under the CGIAR consortium in the 1970s.
Aims to improve food security in semi-arid regions and support smallholder farmers in drylands.
Awarded the Africa Food Prize in 2021 and developed the world's first pigeon pea hybrid.
Launched the ICRISAT Centre of Excellence for South-South Cooperation in collaboration with Research and Information System for Developing Countries (RIS).

title: Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI)
focus: Status, functions, and achievements of SECI in India's renewable energy sector.
rationale: An important national body for renewable energy implementation with specific Navratna status and regulatory roles under CERC.
sectionExcerpt: Established in 2011 for the implementation of the National Solar Mission, SECI is a Navratna Central Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). It is the designated Renewable Energy Implementing Agency (REIA) for MNRE schemes and functions as a Category-I power trading licensee under CERC. SECI implements special programmes like the greening of islands (Lakshadweep, A&N Islands) and high-altitude grid-linked capacity in Ladakh.
evidenceBullets: SECI is a Navratna Central Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
Established in 2011 specifically for the implementation of the National Solar Mission.
Functions as a Category-I (highest) power trading licensee under the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC).
Implements special regional models such as the greening of islands (Lakshadweep, A&N) and a proposed 5 GW high-altitude grid-linked capacity in Ladakh.

title: Related Reports and Indices in News
focus: Key environmental and agricultural reports and their publishing organizations.
rationale: A cluster of miscellaneous but highly testable reports and indices often asked in UPSC prelims as match-the-following questions.
sectionExcerpt: Several key reports were recently released: The Production Gap Report 2025 by the Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI), Climate Analytics, and IISD highlights the discrepancy between governments' planned fossil fuel production and levels consistent with limiting warming to 1.5°C or 2°C. The Global Electricity Mid-Year Insights 2025 was released by the energy think tank Ember, and The State of Food and Agriculture (SOFA) 2025 Report was released by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO).
evidenceBullets: The Production Gap Report 2025 is released by the Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI), Climate Analytics, and IISD.
The 'Production Gap' refers to the discrepancy between planned fossil fuel production and global production levels consistent with limiting warming to 1.5°C or 2°C.
The Global Electricity Mid-Year Insights 2025 is released by the energy think tank Ember.
The State of Food and Agriculture (SOFA) 2025 Report is released by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO).
